name: Tottori Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the Sea of Japan and the steadfast spirit of the prefecture
purity, peace and the bright future of Tottori

description: A deep-blue field charged at the centre with a white emblem composed of the katakana ト・ッ・ト・リ arranged into the form of a flying bird.
history: Tottori's prefectural symbol was adopted on 23 September 1968 to mark the centenary of the Meiji restoration. The flag, with the white emblem on a navy field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: the deep blue of the Sea of Japan along Tottori's northern coast

features.role: Tottori prefectural emblem (stylised katakana トットリ as a flying bird)
features.symbolism: a stylised arrangement of the katakana ト・ッ・ト・リ (To-tto-ri) forming the silhouette of a flying bird, evoking the kanji 鳥 ('bird') in 鳥取 (Tottori) and the prefecture's aspiration for peace and progress.
